title: stillingia zelayensis
intro: Stillingia zelayensis, also known as "Tree of Death" or "Bloodroot", is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and anti-rheumatic effects due to the presence of stillingia root, a compound with potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Stillingia zelayensis are associated with its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, and it has been used to treat various conditions such as arthritis, fever, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Stillingia zelayensis are typically made from the dried root, which can be decocted or used in infusion, and its extracts have been studied for their potential therapeutic applications.
